summary: Reviewed the host-controller tweaks for workflow breakage around pre-game state, first-game detection, and display parity. The happy-path host briefing wiring is mostly present, but two failure paths can silently give the host the wrong briefing and the display refactor has a confirmed text-level parity regression. The iPhone fold target also still needs hard visual verification because the controls are not actually pinned by layout.
appears_sound: The host briefing is gated on `currentGameState.numbers_called_count === 0`, so undoing back to zero will re-show it as specified in `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:785`.

The in-play branch renders nickname before the ball and uses `slide-in-from-top-4`, while omitting the old `Players see this` call-delay hint in `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:793`.

`getColourName()` has a non-empty invalid-input fallback and tests assert `Unknown colour` for empty, malformed, shorthand, and non-hex values in `src/lib/colour-name.ts:28` and `src/lib/colour-name.test.ts:20`.

The specified spacing class changes for card padding, ball size, stats padding, grid gap, button height, and wrapper bottom padding are reflected in `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx`.

findings.description: The display rules refactor is not byte-identical: rule 1 now uses a curly apostrophe, changing the rendered copy from the original ASCII apostrophe. This violates the spec's block-merge parity gate for `/display/[sessionId]`.
findings.evidence: The diff shows the original display JSX used `they're`, while `HOUSE_RULES` now stores `they’re` at `src/lib/house-rules.ts:29`.

findings.description: If the added first-game Supabase read fails, game 1 silently renders without house rules because the error is ignored and `isFirstGameOfSession` falls back to false. Failure sequence: host opens the first game, the extra read errors or returns no row, the page still renders, and the host starts the night without the required rules block.
findings.evidence: `firstGame` is read at `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/page.tsx:71`, but only `data` is captured and `isFirstGameOfSession` is computed as `!!firstGame && ...` at line 79 with no error handling.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The team may intentionally prefer omitting rules over showing them on the wrong game, but the UI gives no indication that required briefing data failed to load.

findings.description: The route can combine a session from one URL segment with a game from another session, which makes first-game detection and the header context wrong. Failure sequence: a stale/manual URL uses `sessionId=A` with `gameId=B`; the page fetches session A, controls game B, and compares game B's index against session A's first game.
findings.evidence: The game query filters only by `id` at `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/page.tsx:39`, while the session and first-game queries use the route `sessionId` at lines 51 and 71.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Normal app-generated links may always pair the IDs correctly, but the server route itself does not enforce that invariant.

findings.description: The code does not actually pin the primary controls; it only caps the briefing body at `55vh` and leaves the card and control grid in normal page flow. On an iPhone-height viewport, the header, card padding, `55vh` body, card margin, and 80px/64px controls can still push Take Break and Check Claim below the fold.
findings.evidence: The briefing wrapper uses `max-h-[55vh] overflow-y-auto` at `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:788`, while the control grid remains a normal-flow sibling after the card at `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:876`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Actual briefing content may be shorter than the cap in the common data set, so only the required iPhone 14 Pro screenshot can settle it.
findings.what_would_confirm: Capture the three required iPhone 14 Pro states and verify the full control grid is visible without page scrolling, especially game 1 with rules.

empty_categories: No new multi-step server mutation was introduced, so no new rollback or idempotency gap was found in this pack.
No new Realtime ordering path was added; existing state freshness handling remains outside the changed logic.
No new external API, payment, webhook, file-upload, or notification failure path was introduced.
No cancellation or unmount cleanup issue was evident in the changed snippets.
